currentSliceTitle: Give recurring hostile commanders target memory and rivalry continuity
currentSliceNotes: Completed a follow-on hostile-commander slice that keeps save-backed target memory and rivalry state on the existing enemy roster, biases later raid targeting through the current enemy-turn and adventure-target rules, surfaces compact continuity hints through existing frontier, town, and battle summaries, and adds focused regression coverage for repeat-target and repeat-opponent continuity.

Implement app boot flow and main menu shell
Implement first overworld shell
Implement first tactical battle shell
Wire save/load and data-driven content definitions
Harden the runnable scaffold and content wiring
Run stack-appropriate validation
Split authored content domains and bootstrap sessions through core rules
Add movement-budgeted overworld interactions, resource sites, and town capture flow
Implement the first town economy, building, recruitment, and hero progression loop
Replace the single-stack battle shell with multi-stack tactical combat rules
Run repository-local validation for the expanded content graph and scene wiring
Add authored scenario objectives and runtime victory-defeat handling
Implement the first enemy overworld pressure turn with raids, pillage, and siege state

Implement hero artifacts, equipment slots, and durable save-backed bonuses

Implement authored spells, hero spellbook state, and mana persistence

notes: Initial repo created at /root/dev/heroes-like.
Engine lock recorded in project.md: Godot 4 stable series with GDScript, JSON content, and JSON save snapshots.
Current run is implementing the first runnable foundation slice across boot, menu, overworld, battle, and persistence.
Foundation implemented as runnable shell: boot -> menu -> overworld -> battle -> save/load -> resume.
Validation completed for JSON payload parsing in repository content; headless Godot runtime not available in environment.
Current run audited the generated scaffold for boot blockers, brittle scene flow, and content id mismatches before expanding scope.
Foundation hardening completed: fixed the missing project icon target, corrected scene node paths, removed hardcoded battle setup, and normalized scenario -> encounter -> army-group references.
Repository-local validation passed for project targets, autoload files, scene signal handlers, and content cross references; a Godot executable is still unavailable in this environment.
This run split gameplay content into factions, heroes, units, army groups, towns, buildings, resource sites, encounters, and scenarios with stable ids and cross-reference validation.
Scenario startup now routes through core rules modules instead of scene-owned state construction.
The overworld now supports movement points, resource site collection, town capture, daily income, building construction, recruit growth, stack recruitment, and hero experience/leveling.
Tactical combat now resolves multi-stack armies with initiative order, ranged and melee actions, defend, retaliation, terrain distance modifiers, and survivor sync back into the overworld army.
Expanded validation passed via tests/validate_repo.py plus Python bytecode compilation; headless Godot is still unavailable in this environment.
This run added authored scenario objectives with session-level victory and defeat state stored directly in save data.
A new enemy overworld turn layer now grows faction pressure, spawns raid encounters, pillages player resources, and escalates siege pressure against player towns.
Main menu and overworld presentation now surface objective progress, hostile pressure, and completed-scenario summaries while keeping simulation logic in scripts/core.
Repository-local validation passed again after expanding the content graph checks to cover objectives and enemy-faction turn data; a Godot executable is still unavailable in this environment.
This run added authored hero artifacts with dedicated content validation and scenario pickup placements.
Hero loadouts now persist through the save boundary with equipment slots, backpack state, and collected artifact caches normalized for older saves.
Artifact bonuses now affect real simulation by modifying overworld movement, daily economy, and battle attack, defense, and initiative from core rules.
Repository-local validation passed again for the artifact content graph and updated scene wiring; a Godot executable is still unavailable in this environment.
This run added authored spell content plus hero template spell references and validator coverage for the new spell domain.
Hero state now persists a spellbook and mana pool through the save boundary, with older sessions normalizing to the authored starting spell list.
The first spell slice now affects real simulation through an overworld movement-restoration spell and tactical battle damage, defense, and initiative spell effects in scripts/core.
Overworld and battle shells now expose spellbook status and spell actions through thin dynamic UI bars while keeping spell resolution in core rules.
Repository-local validation passed again for the spell content graph and updated scene wiring; a Godot executable is still unavailable in this environment.
This run is upgrading AI with directed hostile adventure-map raids and stronger tactical enemy decision-making.

Enemy overworld pressure now routes through directed raid planning, with hostile encounters choosing objective and town targets, marching across the map, and only counting siege pressure when raids actually reach their assigned fronts.

Battle encounters can now author enemy commanders with command and spellbook state, and tactical enemy turns score spell casts, ranged fire, melee closes, and wounded-target focus through dedicated core AI rules.
Repository-local validation passed again after adding the directed AI slice and encounter commander content; no godot4 or godot executable is available in this environment.
This run is implementing a first dedicated town visit screen plus mage-guild style town spell access on top of the existing economy and spell systems.
The overworld shell now uses a sectioned command layout with banner, command wing, frontier watch, context, and map panels instead of a stacked debug readout.
Release-facing overworld status, scout-net, objective board, frontier watch, active-tile context, and dispatch messaging are now shaped in OverworldRules.gd so the scene controller stays thin.
Repository-local validation now fails if the overworld shell loses its required layout nodes or stops calling the new core summary helpers.

Owned towns now route into a dedicated town scene backed by TownRules, keeping build, recruit, and study actions out of the overworld controller while preserving the same save-backed simulation state.
Town and building content now author tiered spell libraries and spell-tier structures, letting developed towns teach additional field and battle spells directly into the hero spellbook.
Repository-local validation passed again for the town screen, town-route scene wiring, and mage-guild content graph; no godot4 or godot executable is available in this environment.

This run is implementing a reusable scenario scripting and hooks layer on top of objective state so authored events can grant rewards, mutate map state, and react to campaign progress without scene-owned logic.
Scenario content now supports declarative script hooks with save-backed fired-state, event logs, and reusable conditions for day thresholds, town ownership, encounter clears, and objective progress.
The River Pass scenario now authors triggered rewards, relief events, spawned counterattacks, and town/map mutations through core rules instead of scene callbacks.

Repository-local validation passed again after adding the scenario scripting slice and expanding validator coverage for authored hook conditions/effects; no godot4 or godot executable is available in this environment.
This run is adding campaign-grade progression scaffolding with persistent chapter unlock state, scenario completion records, and carryover bundles that can feed future scenarios.

Campaign progression is now backed by authored content in content/campaigns.json, with a two-chapter Reedfall campaign that unlocks Causeway Stand after River Pass and imports resources, hero progression, spells, artifacts, and carryover flags into the downstream scenario.

Repository-local validation now derives required content files from ContentService.gd and validates campaign startability, chapter references, unlock requirements, and carryover wiring; python3 tests/validate_repo.py passes, while no godot4 or godot executable is available in this environment.
This run is hardening hero artifact inventory state so pickups, scripted rewards, and campaign carryover stay duplicate-safe while equip, unequip, and swap flow through canonical core rules.

ArtifactRules.gd now canonicalizes unique artifact ownership across equipped slots and pack inventory, while OverworldRules.gd and TownRules.gd expose the same swap-safe management actions without moving state logic into scene controllers.

Scenario scripting now supports direct award_artifact effects through the same duplicate-safe artifact claim path, and repository-local validation also checks scripted artifact awards plus repeated authored artifact nodes within a scenario.
This run is replacing the blind single-slot save flow with structured manual slots, autosave support, hardened resume normalization, and a real main-menu save browser.
SaveService.gd now manages three manual expedition slots plus autosave, exposes slot metadata summaries for UI, and validates loaded payloads before resuming them into runtime session state.

Resume flow now tolerates older saves, rejects missing-scenario or newer-version payloads, and safely drops broken battle or town resume state back to an overworld session instead of trusting raw JSON blindly.

MainMenu.tscn now surfaces a real save browser with slot inspection and load gating, while overworld and town scenes select an explicit manual slot for saves and autosave on scene transitions plus day advancement.
Repository-local validation passed again after adding save-management API checks and save-browser scene wiring; no godot4 or godot executable is available in this environment.

This run is implementing a distinct skirmish browser and pre-launch setup flow while keeping campaign chapter starts, save-browser resume, and progression semantics on the existing durable bootstrap path.

The main menu now separates campaign actions from a skirmish browser, uses authored scenario selection metadata plus a real difficulty setup before launch, and persists skirmish launch mode in session/save summaries so campaign progression is not mutated by skirmish replays.

Repository-local validation passes for the skirmish slice via python3 tests/validate_repo.py and Python bytecode compilation; no godot4 or godot executable is available in this environment for a headless engine smoke test.
This run is hardening difficulty so the saved session.difficulty field drives real overworld economy and pressure plus tactical combat resolution instead of staying menu-only metadata.

DifficultyRules.gd now centralizes profile data for story, normal, and hard, and fresh or restored sessions derive movement, income, reward, raid, initiative, and damage modifiers from that shared core path without changing save version 9.

Repository-local validation now checks difficulty integration tokens across ScenarioFactory, OverworldRules, EnemyTurnRules, EnemyAdventureRules, BattleRules, and BattleAiRules; no godot4 or godot executable is available in this environment for a headless engine smoke test.

This run finished the multi-hero command slice by hardening save-backed player roster normalization, Wayfarers Hall hiring, town-only hero switching, garrison transfer, secondary-hero defeat cleanup, and primary-hero campaign carryover handling in scripts/core.

Overworld and town shells now expose thin command, tavern, and transfer surfaces instead of leaving the slice core-only, and the overworld map marks active versus secondary hero positions for switching context.
Repository-local validation passes for the expanded hero-command slice via python3 tests/validate_repo.py, including the new content assumptions and overworld/town UI wiring checks.

This run made multi-hero exploration materially matter by adding save-backed overworld fog state, explored-memory normalization for legacy saves, and per-hero scouting coverage aggregated from progression and artifacts in scripts/core.

OverworldShell.gd now abstracts unseen tiles, suppresses hidden town/resource/artifact/encounter information, and surfaces exploration progress while still rendering active and secondary hero positions through visible scouting coverage.

Repository-local validation passes for the fog/scouting slice via python3 tests/validate_repo.py, including checks for save-version preservation, normalization hooks, scripted visibility gating, and overworld UI wiring.
This run is deepening tactical combat with authored unit abilities, status interactions, and ability-aware AI on top of the current abstract distance-based battle model.

Tactical combat now authors reach, brace, harry, backstab, shielding, and volley directly in units content, with durable round-to-round status effects normalized through SpellRules and applied in BattleRules instead of placeholder text.
Battle AI now values status setup and payoff, while the battle shell stays thin by rendering rule-provided roster summaries and disabling unavailable actions through core-owned action availability.
Repository-local validation passed for the combat-depth slice via python3 tests/validate_repo.py, including unit-ability schema, battle/status tokens, and battle UI wiring.

This run deepened towns into authored faction engines with economy profiles, dwelling upgrade chains, weekly muster growth, normalized prerequisite graphs, and blocker-aware construction surfaced through thin town UI.

Repository-local validation passed for the town/faction progression slice via python3 tests/validate_repo.py, including schema checks for faction/town/building progression plus source checks for weekly-muster and town-shell wiring.

This run broadened campaign progression into a three-chapter authored Reedfall chain, adding a new downstream finale with unlock and carryover wiring instead of stopping at the previous thin default flow.

MainMenu.tscn and MainMenu.gd now expose a release-facing campaign browser with campaign selection, chapter status, retry/start actions, and authored carryover context driven by CampaignProgression.gd and CampaignRules.gd rather than menu-owned progression logic.

Save summaries now prefer explicit campaign session flags, and repository-local validation passed again after adding checks for campaign chapter metadata, browser hooks, and campaign-browser scene wiring.

This run added a dedicated SettingsService autoload with its own user config file, keeping presentation, audio, and accessibility preferences separate from campaign progression and expedition save data.
MainMenu.tscn and MainMenu.gd now use a tabbed release-facing shell with play, guide, settings, and saves views, including help topics for campaign, skirmish, overworld, town, battle, and save flow.
Repository-local validation passed again after adding checks for the settings autoload registration, persistence tokens, onboarding topics, and menu settings-help hooks.

This run added a dedicated ScenarioOutcomeShell and rewired resolved sessions through AppRouter so campaign and skirmish results land on a real recap flow instead of dropping straight back to the menu.

ScenarioRules.gd now builds shared post-scenario recap models and follow-up actions, while CampaignRules.gd surfaces chapter records, downstream unlock state, and carryover import-export consequences from the real campaign profile.

Resolved sessions now overwrite autosave before the result shell opens, and repository-local validation passed again after adding checks for outcome routing, recap builders, and result-shell scene wiring.
This run turned hostile factions into real overworld empires with per-faction treasury, posture, building, recruitment, and reinforcement decisions kept in scripts/core instead of scene controllers.

Enemy raid encounters now normalize dynamic enemy-army payloads for legacy saves, letting weekly musters and town recruitment materially increase raid pillage pressure and downstream battle strength without changing save version 9.
Repository-local validation passed again after adding checks for enemy empire state, dynamic raid-army battle wiring, and visibility-safe threat surfacing.

This run converted hostile town pressure into queued defense battles by extending the existing encounter battle flow with town-defense context, defender commander sources, and post-battle raid-garrison syncing.

Town assaults now resolve through real combat even when the primary hero is elsewhere, falling back to a town-captain commander payload and routing battle entry through the same thin AppRouter/BattleShell path as normal encounters.
Repository-local validation passed again after adding checks for town-defense battle routing, battle-context sync hooks, and town-loss consequence handling.

This run polished the town shell into a release-facing management surface with a sectioned layout, wrapped action controls, and dedicated hall, defense, frontier-watch, and dispatch panels instead of a single stacked debug column.

TownRules.gd now shapes richer town-facing summaries for identity, construction ledgers, recruit reserves, stationed defenders, visibility-safe local raid pressure, and clearer order-result dispatch text so the scene controller only binds labels and actions.
Repository-local validation, Python bytecode compilation, and JSON parsing all passed again after adding town-shell polish coverage for the new layout nodes and core summary hooks.
This run broadened the authored campaign package from a single Reedfall arc into two full three-chapter arcs by adding the new Stonewake campaign path led by Caelen Ashgrove.

The new Stonewake scenarios add original maps, towns, encounter chains, carryover flag wiring, and browser-visible metadata while reusing the existing CampaignRules and CampaignProgression flows instead of adding menu-owned branches.

CampaignRules.gd now advertises region, chapter count, and victory progress directly in campaign-browser summaries, and repository-local validation now enforces at least two authored campaign arcs with at least six total chapters.

This run polished the battle shell into a release-facing tactical surface with commander boards, initiative visibility, active-stack and target context, effect surfacing, recent combat dispatch, and clearer action guidance while keeping scene mutation thin.

BattleRules.gd now owns battle-shell summaries plus durable recent event feed shaping, SpellRules.gd now authors spell-action summaries, and BattleShell.gd reuses the expedition save controls without changing save version 9.

Repository-local validation, Python bytecode compilation, and JSON parsing all passed again after adding battle-shell release polish coverage for the new layout nodes, summary helpers, and save-control wiring.

This run hardened release-facing save/load integrity with live slot reinspection, service-owned save timestamps, explicit resume targets, and stronger partial-payload guardrails in SaveService.gd instead of scene controllers.

Valid saves now distinguish expedition resume versus outcome review, manual-slot and autosave summaries surface blocked or recovered state clearly, and stale slot metadata no longer drives restore decisions.

Repository-local validation, Python bytecode compilation, and JSON parsing all passed again after adding save-integrity coverage for service guardrails, menu button state, and restore-path assumptions.

This run deepened hostile map pressure so enemy raids can contest authored resource sites, relic caches, neutral encounter fronts, retake-sensitive towns, and objective anchors through the same save-backed overworld state the player uses.

Repository-local validation, Python bytecode compilation, and JSON parsing all passed again after adding strategic-enemy coverage for contestable map targets, persisted node metadata, and visibility-safe overworld threat surfacing.

This run moved active-session saving into a shared runtime path so overworld, town, battle, and outcome shells all expose manual-slot saves, latest-ready context, and safe return-to-menu behavior without owning persistence logic themselves.

Repository-local validation, Python bytecode compilation, and JSON parsing all passed again after adding in-session save coverage for router-driven resume flow, shell save surfaces, and resolved-outcome manual save support.

This run expanded hero content into a broader authored roster with progression-seeded specialties, faction-facing identity summaries, and summary helpers that keep recruitment and setup surfaces thin.

A new Mireclaw campaign arc plus a Mira-led skirmish front now broaden campaign and skirmish variety through the existing content model, while validator coverage enforces multi-faction campaign starts, skirmish-only fronts, and distinct lead-hero breadth.

This run deepened authored scenario identity with additional encounter-clearing side objectives, reactive counterattacks, relief events, enemy-pressure spikes, and faction-specific neutral encounter mixes across the existing campaign and skirmish fronts.

ScenarioScriptRules.gd now supports objective-not-met, raid-count, hook-dependency, and add_enemy_pressure hooks, while OverworldRules.gd surfaces recent scripted beats as a scenario pulse so chapter pressure stays legible without scene-owned mutation.

Repository-local validation, Python bytecode compilation, and JSON parsing all passed again after adding authored-scenario identity coverage for reactive hooks, encounter side objectives, scripted spawns, and dispatch-visible event surfacing.

This run deepened town progression with advanced faction-specific buildings, stronger economy and recruitment asymmetry, and late-game outputs that materially change reserve quality, town readiness, and hostile pressure gain.

This run started a narrow hotfix for a confirmed recursion overflow in OverworldRules.gd where command-risk forecast shaping re-entered normalize_overworld_state() through get_active_context() while the active hero was moving on the overworld.

The recursion hotfix stayed local to OverworldRules.gd by replacing the command-risk field item's town-context check with _find_active_town(session), avoiding the normalize_overworld_state() re-entry while preserving the same town suppression behavior.
